steps.title: Welcome to Pulsar!
steps.description:
Pulsar is a radial launcher built around muscle memory. Here's what to learn first:

1. Task Mode `Ctrl+Q`: Quickly switch or launch common apps
2. Action Mode `Ctrl+Shift+Q`: Execute command slots in the current context
3. Settings > Slots: All slots are configured here

This tutorial will guide you through the shortest loop: open settings, add a switch slot, then add an action slot.

steps.title: Open Settings
steps.description:
First, open the settings window. The rest of the tutorial will focus on the Slots page inside Settings.

You can click "Open Settings" below, or open it from the Pulsar tray icon.
steps.waitHintText: After clicking "Open Settings", the tutorial will continue automatically. You can also open settings via the tray icon.

steps.title: Understanding Settings
steps.description:
Pulsar's Settings has three main sections:

• General: Hotkeys, themes, and startup behavior
• Slots: Configure Task Mode / Action Mode slots
• Plugins: View and manage installed plugins

This tutorial focuses on the Slots page, since it determines what appears in the radial menu.

steps.title: Go to Slots
steps.description:
Click "Slots" in the left navigation.

You'll see a Context/Profile switcher at the top:

• `Launcher`: Switch slots used in Task Mode
• `Global`: Action slots visible in all applications
• Other Profiles: Action slots visible only in specific apps

Start with `Launcher` and `Global` — they're the most stable and easy to understand.
steps.waitHintText: Click "Slots" in the left nav and the tutorial will continue. You can also click "Continue" manually.

steps.title: Create a Task Mode Slot
steps.description:
Now add a `Window Switcher` slot under the `Launcher` context.

Recommended values:
• app: `notepad`
• path: `C:\Windows\System32\notepad.exe`
• Display Label: `Notepad`

Why this first?
• Easiest to verify: press `Ctrl+Q` to see it
• Window Switcher handles both switching and launching
• This is the most core Pulsar slot type

Save your changes when done.
steps.waitHintText: After adding the slot, the tutorial will continue automatically. You can also click "Continue".

steps.title: Test Task Mode
steps.description:
Now verify that your config actually appears in the radial menu.

How to test:
1. Hold `Ctrl+Q` to open Task Mode
2. Check if your "Notepad" slot appears in the ring
3. Release onto the slot — Pulsar will switch to or launch Notepad

The key takeaway: the `Launcher` context = Task Mode content.
steps.waitHintText: Trigger Task Mode once and the tutorial will continue. If not detected, verify your hotkey, or click Continue.

steps.title: Create a Global Action Slot
steps.description:
Back in the Slots page, switch the context to `Global` and add a `Command` slot.

Recommended values:
• path: `notepad.exe`
• arguments: `C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts`
• Display Label: `Open Hosts File`

Why `Global` instead of an app profile?
• More stable for first-time setup, not dependent on active window
• When you press `Ctrl+Shift+Q`, this slot appears in every app
• Once you're comfortable, create app-specific profiles naturally
steps.waitHintText: Switch to Global context and add a Command slot to continue. You can also click Continue manually.

steps.title: Test Action Mode
steps.description:
Finally, verify Action Mode.

How to test:
1. Hold `Ctrl+Shift+Q` to open Action Mode
2. Check if your `Global` command slot appears
3. Release onto the slot to execute the command

Key understanding:
• `Global` slots appear in every application
• Later, if you create a profile for a specific app, Action Mode overlays or swaps its slots
steps.waitHintText: Trigger Action Mode once and the tutorial will continue. If not detected, verify your hotkey is still Ctrl+Shift+Q.

steps.title: 🎉 Congratulations! Tutorial Complete
steps.description:
You've completed Pulsar's minimum onboarding loop:

✅ Configured a Task Mode slot in `Launcher`
✅ Verified app switching/launching with `Ctrl+Q`
✅ Configured an Action Mode slot in `Global`
✅ Verified command execution with `Ctrl+Shift+Q`

What's next:
• Create dedicated profiles for your frequently used apps
• Add clearer icons, names, and parameters to your slots
• Explore PKI, VBA Runner, Bookmarklet, and other plugins
• Adjust hotkeys and themes to your preference

If you remember one thing: start with `Launcher` and `Global`, and Pulsar is already production-ready.
